title: DataStream: The Real-Time Analytics Dashboard
description: Build a data processing engine that takes a raw stream of events (logs, clicks, transactions) and transforms them into a structured analytics dashboard. You will use advanced array methods to group, filter, and aggregate data in real-time.
requirements: A data generator that produces a random array of 1,000 'event' objects (type, timestamp, value).
A filtering system to isolate events by 'type' (e.g., only 'Purchase' events).
An aggregation engine using .reduce() to calculate total revenue, average order value, and total event count.
A transformation pipeline using .map() to format raw timestamps into human-readable dates.
A sorting mechanism to display the 'Top 5' highest-value events.
A function to detect 'anomalies' (events with values 3x the average) using .filter().

milestones.title: Data Pipeline Basics
tasks: Create the basic filter and map functions to clean the data

milestones.title: The Reduction Engine
tasks: Build a complex .reduce() function that calculates multiple metrics (sum, count, avg) in a single pass
Validate the math against a small known dataset

milestones.title: Advanced Analytics
tasks: Format the final result into a clean report object

stretchGoals: Implement 'Windowing' (calculating metrics for the last 5 minutes only).
Add a 'Grouping' function that turns the array into an object keyed by event type { 'Purchase': [...], 'Click': [...] } using reduce.
Visualize the final data using a simple HTML table with color-coded anomaly rows.

evaluationRubric.efficiency: Does the processing pipeline avoid unnecessary iterations (e.g., chaining filter and map efficiently)?
evaluationRubric.accuracy: Are the aggregation metrics mathematically correct?
evaluationRubric.codeHygiene: Is the logic split into small, testable pure functions?
